title: Goroutine Pools, Context Causes, Backpressure, and Shutdown · Go Semantics Case: Error Aggregation
summary: A goroutine is launched per URL, workers close shared channels, cancellation stops fetch but not parsing or writes, and main returns before cleanup. Predict owner authority, URL and HTTP state, Go ownership, bounded work, accessibility impact, failure, cleanup, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the crawler go error aggregation increment in bounded leak-free Go crawl pipeline. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case pra…
objectives: Collect per-item outcomes without abandoning siblings, leaking goroutines, hiding first cause, or converting policy stops into errors.
Derive owner, run, item, attempt, deadline, and shutdown contexts and preserve the first meaningful cancellation cause.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
